follow_up_workstreams.name: performance
follow_up_workstreams.reason: Benchmark high-volume DATA/PROC SQL jobs and decide when pandas should be replaced by SQL pushdown, Polars, Dask, or Spark.

follow_up_workstreams.name: validation
follow_up_workstreams.reason: Compare SAS and Python outputs with row counts, schemas, checksums, aggregates, statistical tolerances, and edge-case fixtures.

follow_up_workstreams.name: workflow_integration
follow_up_workstreams.reason: Connect generated Python jobs into the target scheduler, logging, documentation, and downstream data contracts.

follow_up_workstreams.name: team_enablement
follow_up_workstreams.reason: Use reports and IR artifacts for code review, knowledge transfer, and post-migration ownership.

follow_up_workstreams.name: llm_assisted_review
follow_up_workstreams.reason: Optional: use LLMs for explanation, documentation, test generation, and manual-review acceleration, while keeping deterministic compiler output as the source of truth.
